refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency metric (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal jitter and packet loss.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking data exchange with low overhead.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and optimized data encoding.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to preprocess telemetry data near Vehicle.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic on the network link with QoS mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and optimized data encoding.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between Vehicle telemetry generation and Teleoperation data consumption.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates latency reduction to communication protocols and encoding, minimizing cross-module dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol incompatibility with legacy Vehicle systems.
Risk of increased complexity in protocol implementation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms or adaptive protocols to handle network variability.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for implementing communication protocols, encoding/decoding, and managing telemetry and tasking data streams.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional telemetry data exchange between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Command and control tasking data exchanged between Teleoperation and Vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting data transmission.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on communication latency specifics.
Generates telemetry but does not control communication protocols end-to-end.
Primary responsibility for communication protocol implementation and latency management.
Focuses on safety alerts, not communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP to Teleoperation module centralizes latency management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for telemetry data format compatibility.
Risk of interface overload if Teleoperation module handles too many responsibilities.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface complexity and consider splitting Teleoperation module if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear latency reduction mechanisms and measurable criteria.

diffusion_risks: Teleoperation module may become a bottleneck if communication protocol complexity grows.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor Teleoperation module, supporting clear ownership and manageable diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry interface measurements and network monitoring tools.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear mapping from FR to DP to IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback or adaptive communication strategies in DP to handle network variability.
Review Teleoperation module interface load in future decompositions.

decision.justification: While the decomposition is sound, risks related to network variability and interface complexity warrant refinement before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal jitter and packet loss.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking data exchange with low overhead.

revise.why: Incorporate adaptive communication protocols and fallback mechanisms to mitigate network variability risks.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Vehicle telemetry data format is compatible with Teleoperation protocols.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under varied network conditions.
